headline: Ancient Egyptian Mummy Reveals Homer's Iliad Papyrus
meta_description: A 1,600-year-old Egyptian mummy contained a fragment of Homer's Iliad, marking the first known evidence of Greek literature intentionally used in mummification.
summary_short: Archaeologists discovered a fragment of Homer's Iliad inside a 1,600-year-old Egyptian mummy, offering a first glimpse of Greek texts in ancient burial practices.
body:
When Egyptian archaeologists unwrapped a 1,600-year-old mummy, they made a discovery that surprised even the most seasoned researchers: a papyrus fragment containing part of Homer's Iliad, specifically the famous "Catalogue of Ships" section. The text had been deliberately placed within the wrappings during the mummification process.

This find is unprecedented. It marks the first archaeological evidence that ancient Egyptians intentionally incorporated Greek literature into mummification rituals, revealing a fascinating cultural bridge between two ancient civilizations. The discovery enriches our understanding of how different cultures exchanged ideas, honored their dead, and valued the written word across centuries and boundaries.
